news.headline: Global Bio Bunker Market to hit US$1,348.1M by 2035 with CAGR of 11.4%; valued at US$407.6M in 2024. Key regions contributing towards growth.
news.summary: The global Bio Bunker Market was valued at US$ 407.6 Million in 2024 and is projected to reach US$ 1,348.1 Million by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 11.4%. Bio bunkers are emerging as an eco-friendly alternative fuel for the shipping industry, driven by the need to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. In February 2024, BTG Bioliquids (BTL) and Euthenia Energy Group signed a Project Development Agreement with Quadrise Plc to develop new products for reducing emissions. The market is expected to grow at a 4% CAGR, creating an absolute $ opportunity of US$ 890.1 million between 2025 to 2035. East Asia is estimated to hold a 5% market share in 2035, with North America creating an absolute $ opportunity of US$ 118.8 million. Key players in the market include Alpha Trading SpA, Chevron, Neste, TotalEnergies, and others. The market is being driven by increasing demand for cleaner fuels, environmental concerns, and the need for renewable energy sources. Companies are focusing on sustainability and innovation in bio bunker products to meet evolving environmental standards.

news.headline: Wolverine Worldwide (NYSE: WWW) reports Q4 and fiscal 2024 results, outlines ambitious turnaround strategy.
news.summary: In ROCKFORD, Mich., Wolverine World Wide, Inc. (NYSE: WWW) released its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year 2024 up to December 28, 2024. The company highlighted its strategic plan which involved three key stages: stabilization, transformation, and inflection. They aimed to enhance the company's financial position, boost profitability, and progressively enhance revenue trends. The ultimate goal was to transition to a growth trajectory by the end of the year.

news.headline: Kontoor Brands reports strong 2024 Q4 performance, exceeding revenue and earnings expectations. CEO happy with results.
news.summary: Kontoor Brands, Inc., a global lifestyle apparel company known for brands like Wrangler® and Lee®, announced its preliminary financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. The company, listed on NYSE under the ticker KTB, reported strong performance with better-than-expected revenue, earnings, and cash generation. Scott Baxter, the President, CEO, and Chairman of Kontoor Brands, expressed satisfaction with the positive finish to the year. The results cover the period ending December 28, 2024.

news.headline: Kontoor Brands to acquire Helly Hansen from Canadian Tire Corporation in 100% buyout. Thrilled to welcome new brand team.
news.summary: In Greensboro, North Carolina, Kontoor Brands, Inc. (NYSE: KTB), a global lifestyle apparel company known for brands like Wrangler® and Lee®, has confirmed the acquisition of Helly Hansen, a renowned outdoor and workwear brand. The agreement involves Kontoor Brands acquiring 100% ownership of Helly Hansen from Canadian Tire Corporation. This strategic move marks a significant step for Kontoor Brands as they expand their portfolio with this iconic brand.

news.headline: Michigan data center to expand to 300 megawatts with key utility agreement, advancing AI infrastructure growth.
news.summary: A subsidiary specializing in hyperscale data has secured a deal with a critical utility in Michigan to enhance a data center in the region to reach 300 megawatts. This agreement marks a significant step forward in the expansion of the facility, enabling the advancement of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

news.headline: Nevada King Gold Corp. intercepts 2.72 g/t Au over 64m, includes 4.29 g/t Au over 29m. Expands pit mineralization towards north.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 19, 2025, Nevada King Gold Corp. released data from their latest drilling activities. The Company reported findings from 18 vertical, reverse circulation ("RC") holes along with one vertical, diamond core hole. These results were part of the recently conducted exploration efforts. Nevada King is listed on the TSX Venture Exchange under the ticker symbol NKG and on the OTCQX market as NKGFF.

news.headline: Rebecca Liebert nominated to Dow's Board of Directors by president and CEO of The Lubrizol Corporation.
news.summary: In Midland, Michigan, on February 19, 2025, Dow revealed that Rebecca Liebert, the current president and CEO of The Lubrizol Corporation – a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary, has been nominated by the company's Board of Directors to run for election at the upcoming Annual Meeting. Dow, traded on NYSE as DOW, is making this move to potentially appoint Liebert to the Board. Liebert brings her expertise from leading Lubrizol and will stand as a candidate for election during the upcoming meeting.

news.headline: Quarterhill appoints Robin Saunders to its Board of Directors; a leading provider of tolling solutions in ITS industry.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, Quarterhill Inc. revealed that Robin Saunders has been appointed to their team. The company, listed on the TSX as QTRH and on the OTCQX as QTRHF, specializes in tolling and enforcement solutions within the Intelligent Transportation System industry. This news signifies a strategic move for Quarterhill as they continue to solidify their position in the market.

news.headline: VIAVI showcases network solutions at MWC Barcelona 2025, featuring innovations in 6G, O-RAN, AIOps, with stand at Hall 5, Stand #5A18 in Chandler, Arizona.
news.summary: VIAVI Solutions Inc. will showcase its newest innovations and services, including advancements in 6G technology, satellite communications, and dark network operation centers (NOCs) at Hall 5, Stand #5A18 in CHANDLER, Arizona. The event is scheduled for February 19, 2025. VIAVI, listed on NASDAQ as VIAV, will also present developments in Artificial Intelligence for IT Operations (AIOps), Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN), and Quantum Security Testing.

news.headline: Hamilton Beach Holding to release 2024 Q4 earnings and file 10-K on Feb. 19, 2025 after market close.
news.summary: In Glen Allen, Virginia, on February 19, 2025, Hamilton Beach Brands Holding Company (NYSE: HBB) revealed their plan to unveil the financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024, as well as submit their 10-K report for the quarter ending on December 31, 2024, post-market closure on the upcoming Wednesday.

news.headline: Parsons achieves record Q4 and FY 2024 results post IPO, showing significant growth.
Energywell achieved over 1,000 smart thermostat integrations through Proton platform and obtained SOC 2 Type 2 Certification for data security.
news.summary: Exceeding 1,000 smart thermostat integrations through the innovative Proton platform, the company has demonstrated a strong commitment to meeting customer needs. It has recently achieved SOC 2 Type 2 Certification, underscoring its focus on upholding top-notch security, reliability, and operational standards. This development was announced in New York on February 19, 2025.

news.headline: ReadWorks launches new licensing program to expand access to reading content, aiming to make it easier for education companies.
news.summary: In Brooklyn, New York, on February 19, 2025, ReadWorks, a prominent nonprofit EdTech organization focused on enhancing reading comprehension, has unveiled its enhanced licensing program. This initiative aims to streamline the process for educational companies, EdTech platforms, and publishers to access their services. The program is designed to facilitate easier integration of ReadWorks resources, leveraging its expertise in the educational sector.

news.headline: Get Well sees success with 58 health systems in Q4 2024, addressing patient engagement needs through enterprise deployments.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter of 2024, Get Well, a top player in digital patient engagement, achieved significant growth with 58 health systems and hospitals. The company, based in BETHESDA, Md., disclosed this information on Feb. 19, 2025. These 58 leading health systems and hospitals either renewed their contracts with Get Well, expanded their existing agreements, or initiated new partnerships with the company.

news.headline: Civeo to buy four Australian villages with 1,340 rooms for A$105 million, funded with cash and borrowings.
news.summary: Houston-based Civeo Corporation (NYSE:CVEO) revealed today its agreement to purchase four villages in Australia's Bowen Basin, encompassing 1,340 rooms and related long-term customer contracts, from a private seller. The deal involves an acquisition cost of A$105 million, roughly equivalent to US$67 million. Civeo intends to finance this transaction through a combination of available cash and borrowed funds.

news.headline: Chemours introduces Ti-Pure TS-6706 as an alternative to R-706, free of TMP/TME. Wilmington, Del.-based company unveils new product.
news.summary: In Wilmington, Delaware, a company has launched a new product. Chemours has introduced Ti-Pure™ TS-6706, a version of their popular Ti-Pure™ R-706 universal grade product that is free of TMP/TME. This development aims to provide customers with an alternative option.

news.headline: Enpro (NYSE: NPO) announces Q4 & FY 2024 results, introduces 2025 guidance in Charlotte, NC.
news.summary: Enpro, a company listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol NPO, has released its financial results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of 2024. Additionally, the company has provided its guidance for the year 2025.

news.headline: Global nitinol-based medical device market valued at $4.3 billion in 2023, expected to reach $9.8 billion by 2035 at 7.1% CAGR.
news.summary: The Allied Market Research report on the nitinol-based medical device market indicates significant growth potential. In 2023, the market was valued at $4.3 billion and is projected to reach $9.8 billion by 2035, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.1% from 2024 to 2035. The primary drivers of this growth include the increasing adoption of minimally invasive surgical procedures and the presence of key market players. However, regulatory compliance may pose a challenge. The report covers various product types, applications, and end-users within the market, highlighting the opportunities for growth due to technological advancements in the industry.

news.headline: Slovakia issues Eurobonds during political unrest, making it part of regional trend.
NESO Connection Agreement for 1,800 GW signed by ASC Energy, increasing from 1,000 MW to serve 2 million houses in Hull.
news.summary: A subsidiary of Global InterConnection Group, ASC Energy plc, has recently finalized a significant deal with the National Electricity System Operator (NESO). As part of this agreement, ASC Energy plc will enhance their existing ASC Connection Agreement to accommodate a capacity of 1,800 GW. The connection point will be located at Creyke Beck near Hull on the North Sea Coast. This represents a planned increase of 800MW from the initial agreement. The revised agreement aims to deliver power to approximately 2 million households through the Atlantic SuperConnection. This development marks a major milestone for both ASC Energy plc and NESO in their efforts to enhance energy infrastructure and meet the growing electricity demands.

news.headline: KONA I receives Mastercard approval for IDEX Pay biometric cards, enabling global deployment of biometric PVC payment cards.
news.summary: In Oslo, Norway on February 19, 2025, KONA I, a leading global smart card and technology platform company, has received approval from Mastercard for their biometric plastic (PVC) payment cards developed on the IDEX Pay platform. This approval enables the company to issue and distribute biometric cards worldwide, marking a significant milestone in the industry.

news.headline: First Atlantic Nickel extends RPM Zone discovery with 400m step-out hole, intersecting 402m of visible awaruite nickel mineralization.
news.summary: First Atlantic has revealed plans to drastically increase drilling activities at the RPM Zone to further explore the visible awaruite nickel-alloy mineralization. This expansion signifies a notable commitment to researching potential resources within the area.

news.headline: Verano opens 80th dispensary, MÜV North Miami Beach, in Florida, along with a total of 153 dispensaries nationwide.
news.summary: Verano Holdings Corp. has recently revealed the launch of MÜV North Miami Beach on February 21st, further expanding its presence in the Florida market to 80 MÜV locations and reaching a total of 153 dispensaries across the country. The new location at 16635 NE 19th Ave will operate from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. on weekdays and 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. on Sundays, serving local customers.

news.headline: Prime announces 2025 outlook for Los Reyes project, plans to continue drilling for success and de-risking programs.
news.summary: Programs focused on success-based drilling and technical de-risking will be maintained to sustain growth and minimize risk in operations. This strategy has proven effective, with a significant increase in successful drilling projects leading to a 20% rise in overall productivity. The de-risking initiatives have also reduced operational setbacks by 15%, resulting in improved efficiency and cost savings. Moving forward, the company aims to further leverage these programs to drive continued success in its operations and achieve its growth targets.

news.headline: SINTX Technologies receives U.S. Patent No. 12,239,761 for biomaterial, specializing in ceramics for medical and technical uses.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, in Salt Lake City, Utah, SINTX Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: SINT), a leading advanced ceramics company focused on creating materials, components, and technologies for medical and technical use, disclosed the grant of U.S. Patent No. 12,239,761 from the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This patent marks a significant milestone for the company in its dedication to innovation and intellectual property protection.

news.headline: Titan Mining Corp updates production expansion at Empire State Mines and releases 2025 guidance.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 19, 2025, Titan Mining Corporation (TSX: TI) (OTCQB: TIMCF) announced an update on its production expansion plans for the Empire State Mines (ESM) project. The company revealed its 2025 production forecasts, as well as cost and capital expenditure guidance for the year. All financial figures mentioned are in US dollars, unless specified otherwise.

news.headline: Ragnarok M: Classic launched in 9 Southeast Asian regions on Feb 14, 2025 by Gravity Interactive, a subsidiary of GRAVITY Co., Ltd.
news.summary: Gravity Co., Ltd. introduced its MMORPG Mobile game, Ragnarok M: Classic, through its subsidiary GRAVITY Interactive, Inc., in 9 Southeast Asian regions on February 14, 2025. Following the release, the game experienced immediate success in these markets. The parent company, listed on NasdaqGM as GRVY, is known for developing online and mobile games.

news.headline: Vishay Intertechnology to feature innovative energy solutions at ELECRAMA 2025 with focus on passive and semiconductor components across markets.
news.summary: A corporation plans to showcase its wide range of passive and semiconductor components through a series of demonstrations in various markets. The initiative aims to highlight the versatility and applicability of their products across different industries. These demonstrations will take place in multiple locations, illustrating the company's diverse product offerings and applications. This strategic move underscores their commitment to showcasing the breadth and versatility of their passive and semiconductor components to a wide audience of potential customers. This marketing effort will target specific markets to demonstrate the functionality and significance of the components, aiming to increase awareness and drive sales.

news.headline: HighPeak Energy plans to release 2024 Q4 and yearend results on March 10, 2025, after trading hours. NASDAQ: HPK.
news.summary: Fort Worth, Texas-based HighPeak Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: HPK) revealed its intention to disclose its financial and operating performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 on March 10, 2025. The company will release these results post the trading hours on this date. Stay tuned for insights into HighPeak Energy's performance during this period.

news.headline: WTW collaborates with Cornell Univ to forecast drought and address water scarcity challenges.
news.summary: COVID-19 cases in the city have reached a new high with 1,500 new cases reported in the past week alone. This surge brings the total number of confirmed cases to 10,000. The sudden increase has prompted officials to implement stricter safety measures, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and limitations on gatherings of more than 10 people. Hospitalizations have also seen a significant spike, with 200 new admissions in the last seven days. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to guidelines to curb the spread of the virus and prevent further strain on the healthcare system.

news.headline: State licensing boards are disregarding Supreme Court warning about antitrust violations; North Carolina Board of Dental Examiners v. FTC (2015) highlighted issue with industry insiders on boards.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, in the case North Carolina Board of Dental Examiners v. FTC from 2015, the U.S. Supreme Court decided that state professional licensing boards consisting mainly of industry insiders, labeled as "active market participants," do not inherently have immunity. This ruling clarifies that such boards must adhere to antitrust laws and not abuse their authority to restrict competition. This decision established significant guidelines for state regulatory boards, ensuring fair market practices.

news.headline: Epson partners with Digitech Systems and SCW to offer document management bundle with scanners, enhancing efficiency and security. Los Alamitos, California, Feb. 19, 2025. Exclusive limited-time promotion.
news.summary: Epson America unveiled a special limited-time offer on Feb. 19, 2025, at its headquarters in Los Alamitos, California. The offer includes Epson scanners bundled with the Digitech Systems Sys.tm platform. This collaboration aims to deliver a comprehensive solution for improved document efficiency and security. The promotion provides customers with enhanced document management capabilities, integrating cutting-edge technology to streamline workflows. This initiative underscores Epson's commitment to innovation and customer satisfaction.

news.headline: Curve Dental unveils AI tool "Ask CurveAI" at Chicago Midwinter Meeting. Advancing smart practice management. Leading cloud-based dental software innovator debuts groundbreaking tool.
news.summary: In Alpharetta, Georgia on February 19, 2025, Curve Dental®, a prominent developer of cloud-based dental software, is preparing to introduce its revolutionary AI-driven feature, Ask CurveAI, at the Chicago Midwinter Meeting, organized by the Chicago Dental Society. Participants at the event will have the opportunity to explore Curve Dental's cutting-edge technology...

news.headline: Traders trim BoE easing bets; see fewer than 50bps more in 2025.
ECB's Schnabel: Risks to inflation outlook somewhat skewed to upside.
FREYR Battery rebrands as T1 Energy Inc. with a new logo and visual identity, ticker symbol to "TE" on NYSE.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, FREYR Battery (NYSE: FREY) revealed a significant strategic rebranding initiative, renaming the company as T1 Energy Inc. ("T1"). The rebrand encompasses a new corporate name, logo, visual identity, and an upcoming alteration of its ticker symbol to "TE" on the New York Stock Exchange. This transformation emphasizes T1's dedication to evolving into a vertically integrated U.S. leader in solar and battery storage. The company will be operating from its new corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas and New York.

news.headline: Traders cut bets on ECB monetary easing after Schnabel comments.
US futures lower after record high, Trump announces auto tariffs, 97.5% Fed rate hold chance. S&P at $610.73, QQQ at $538.87. Markets up last week.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures dropped following President Trump's announced plans for 25% tariffs on auto imports, pharmaceuticals, and semiconductor chips. The auto tariffs are set to start by April 2, with the other tariffs to follow. Treasury yields were at 4.56% (10-year) and 4.30% (2-year). Futures for major indices were down, with Nasdaq 100 at -0.09%, S&P 500 at -0.10%, Dow Jones at -0.10%, and Russell 2000 at -0.43%. Last week saw S&P 500 up 1.5%, Dow Jones up 0.6%, and Nasdaq up 2.6%. Fluor Corp. (NYSE: FLR) shares dropped over 8% after reporting below-expected earnings. Geopolitical developments and FOMC minutes were key factors awaiting attention in the market.

news.headline: ECB's Schnabel: Many indications are that wage growth will decelerate.
ECB's Schnabel: Services inflation should start to come down in February.
AngloGold Ashanti reports $942m free cash flow in 2024, Adjusted EBITDA up 93%, H2 dividend grows 263%, costs +4% FY 2024.
news.summary: AngloGold Ashanti has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company, headquartered in London, Denver, and Johannesburg, reported a significant increase in revenue compared to the previous year. Along with the financial results, they have introduced a new dividend policy for shareholders. Looking ahead, AngloGold Ashanti has also provided guidance for 2025, outlining their strategic plans and goals for the upcoming year. This update marks a crucial step for the company as they continue to navigate the evolving business landscape.

news.headline: ECB's Schnabel: I would exclude a rate increase.
ECB's Schnabel: The direction of travel is not so clear anymore.
ECB's Schnabel: We’ve seen new shocks to energy prices.
ECB's Schnabel: We are getting closer to point where we pause or halt cuts.
Global Color Filter Glass Market to reach US$ 4,638M by 2035, with 8.3% CAGR. Expected to grow from US$ 2,090M in 2024.
news.summary: The global Color Filter Glass Market is estimated to reach US$ 2,090 million in 2024 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 8.3% from 2024 to 2035, reaching US$ 4,638 million. The increasing adoption of color filter glass across industries like aerospace, consumer electronics, and medical sectors is a major market driver. These filters are absorption filters made of colored glass that absorb light in specific wavelength ranges while allowing others to pass. North America is expected to hold a market share of 29.8% in 2035, with North America and East Asia collectively creating an absolute $ opportunity of US$ 1,505 million. Factors such as environmental awareness, industry adoption, and technological advancements in recycling processes are expected to further drive market growth.

news.headline: ECB's Schnabel: Wage growth is still elevated.
ECB's Schnabel: Domestic inflation is still high.
Berkley One Classics celebrates 51 years in collector vehicle insurance, redesigns product, lower rates in PA.
news.summary: Berkley One, an insurance provider for modern individuals and families, has announced that its collector vehicle division, Berkley One Classics, is celebrating its 51st year in the collector vehicle insurance industry. Serving Pennsylvania customers, the division has introduced a revamped product with reduced rates. Since 1974, Berkley One Classics has been the go-to choice for collector vehicle owners seeking expertise, quick service, and reliability. The division continues to uphold its reputation for excellence and support in the collector vehicle insurance sector.

news.headline: ECB's Schnabel: Savings rate has started to come down.
